turns.content: I'm trying to build a tool that processes two separate lists of data, but I keep getting confused about how to merge them. Is there a way to just link the iterators together so they run in sequence without losing any items?

You are dealing with Iterator::chain which is exactly designed for this. Think of it like two conveyor belts feeding into one output belt; you drop all items from the first belt, then immediately start dropping items from the second belt without ever stopping in between.
That analogy helps a bit, but I'm still stuck on how to actually call it. Do I need to write custom code or is there a standard function I can just drop into my loop?

There is no magic drop-in function; you must explicitly use the Iterator::chain method on your two iterators. For example, if you have a slice of strings and another collection, you pass them directly as arguments like iter1.chain(iter2). Then you can iterate over that single combined iterator just like any other.
Okay, so I don't need custom code? Can this handle cases where one of my sources might be empty or contain optional values?

Yes, it handles those gracefully because iterators themselves are already safe against emptiness. If you use std::iter::once for a single value or std::iter::empty for nothing, they still behave as valid iterators that Iterator::chain can link to your other source without crashing.
